qtype: FDP conversion, fraction simplification, fraction of quantity, factors, HCF, irrational numbers, zero index, compound interest
tex:
(a) Write $\dfrac{1}{4}$ as a decimal.
\newline

    

(b) Write $\dfrac{36}{124}$ as a fraction in its lowest terms.
\newline

    

(c) Work out $\dfrac{5}{8}$ of 128.
\newline

    

(d) Write down all the factors of 24.
\newline

    

(e) Find the highest common factor (HCF) of 24 and 108.
\newline

    

(f) Write down an irrational number between 3 and 9.
\newline

    

(g) Write down the value of $25^0$.
\newline

    

(h) \$8400 is invested for 2 years at a rate of 3.5\% per year compound interest.
\newline
\\
     Work out the total amount of interest earned by the end of the 2 years.
\newline

    \$ 

(i) Without using a calculator, work out $2\dfrac{1}{3} + \dfrac{4}{5}$.
\newline
\\
     You must show all your working and give your answer as a mixed number in its simplest form.
\newline

content.content:
Write $\dfrac{1}{4}$ as a decimal.
\newline

Write $\dfrac{36}{124}$ as a fraction in its lowest terms.
\newline

Work out $\dfrac{5}{8}$ of 128.
\newline

Write down all the factors of 24.
\newline

Find the highest common factor (HCF) of 24 and 108.
\newline

Write down an irrational number between 3 and 9.
\newline

Write down the value of $25^0$.
\newline

\$8400 is invested for 2 years at a rate of 3.5\% per year compound interest.
\newline
\\
    \noindent \hspace{1em}Work out the total amount of interest earned by the end of the 2 years.
\newline

    \$ \AnswerLine[][]

Without using a calculator, work out $2\dfrac{1}{3} + \dfrac{4}{5}$.
\newline
\\
    \noindent \hspace{1em}You must show all your working and give your answer as a mixed number in its simplest form.
\newline

tex:
(a) Emma records the number of letters in each word in a sentence.
\newline
    7   1   5   4   2   4   5   3   3   1   2   4
    Find
    (i) (b) the median,

        

(c) the mode,

        

(d) the range.
\newline

        

(e) Jack records the number of letters in 25 words.
\newline
    (i) (f) \begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
                \hline
                Number of letters in a word & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 & 6 \\ \hline
                Number of words             & 3 & 1 & 5 & 8 & 6 & 2 \\ \hline
            \end{tabular}
        Calculate the mean.
\newline

        

(g) Priti picks one of Jack's words at random.
\newline
\\
        Find the probability that this word has 4 or more letters.
\newline

        

(h) The table shows information about the first 90 words in a book.
\newline
    (i) (i) Complete the table.
\newline
        \begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|}
                \hline
                Length of word & Frequency & Pie chart angle \\ \hline
                Very short     & 6         & 24$^\circ$      \\ \hline
                Short          & 34        &                 \\ \hline
                Medium         & 41        &                 \\ \hline
                Long           & 9         &                 \\ \hline
            \end{tabular}

(j) Complete the pie chart to show this information.
\newline
        %\begin{InsertScreenShot}
            %    
            %\end{InsertScreenShot}

qtype: time calculation, time zones, speed-distance-time, currency conversion, percentage increase, ratio, profit
tex:
(a) A plane leaves Karachi at 15 30 to fly to Bangkok.
\newline
\\
    The distance is 3840 km.
\newline
\\
    The plane flies at an average speed of 720 km/h.
\newline
\\
    The local time in Bangkok is 2 hours ahead of the local time in Karachi.
\newline
\\
    Find the local time in Bangkok when the plane arrives.
\newline

    

(b) In Bangkok a watch costs 2610 baht.
\newline
\\
    The exchange rate is \$1 = 34.8 baht.
\newline
\\
    Find the cost of the watch in dollars.
\newline

    \$ 

(c) The price of another watch increases from \$18 to \$19.17.
\newline
\\
    Find the percentage increase in the price of this watch.
\newline

    

(d) Raoul makes a profit when he sells his watch for \$36.
\newline
\\
    The ratio of the price Raoul paid for the watch and the profit he makes is \\
     price paid : profit = 7 : 2.
\newline
\\
    Work out the profit that Raoul makes.
\newline

    \$

content.content:
A plane leaves Karachi at 15 30 to fly to Bangkok.
\newline
\\
    The distance is 3840 km.
\newline
\\
    The plane flies at an average speed of 720 km/h.
\newline
\\
    The local time in Bangkok is 2 hours ahead of the local time in Karachi.
\newline
\\
    Find the local time in Bangkok when the plane arrives.
\newline

In Bangkok a watch costs 2610 baht.
\newline
\\
    The exchange rate is \$1 = 34.8 baht.
\newline
\\
    Find the cost of the watch in dollars.
\newline

    \$ \AnswerLine[][]

The price of another watch increases from \$18 to \$19.17.
\newline
\\
    Find the percentage increase in the price of this watch.
\newline

Raoul makes a profit when he sells his watch for \$36.
\newline
\\
    The ratio of the price Raoul paid for the watch and the profit he makes is \\
    \noindent \hspace{1em}price paid : profit = 7 : 2.
\newline
\\
    Work out the profit that Raoul makes.
\newline

    \$ \AnswerLine[][]

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
    \item A plane leaves Karachi at 15 30 to fly to Bangkok.\\
    The distance is 3840 km.\\
    The plane flies at an average speed of 720 km/h.\\
    The local time in Bangkok is 2 hours ahead of the local time in Karachi.\\
    Find the local time in Bangkok when the plane arrives.

    \AnswerLine[][]
    \Marks{4}

    \item In Bangkok a watch costs 2610 baht.\\
    The exchange rate is \$1 = 34.8 baht.\\
    Find the cost of the watch in dollars.

    \$ \AnswerLine[][]
    \Marks{2}

    \item The price of another watch increases from \$18 to \$19.17.\\
    Find the percentage increase in the price of this watch.

    \AnswerLine[][\%]
    \Marks{3}

    \item Raoul makes a profit when he sells his watch for \$36.\\
    The ratio of the price Raoul paid for the watch and the profit he makes is \\
    \noindent \hspace{1em}price paid : profit = 7 : 2.\\
    Work out the profit that Raoul makes.

    \$ \AnswerLine[][]
    \Marks{2}
\end{parts}

qtype: simplifying algebraic expressions, substitution, solving linear and simultaneous equations, changing subject
tex:
(a) Simplify.
\newline
    $9x - 2y - 5x - y$

    

(b) $P = 4ab + 3b^2$\\
    Work out the value of $a$ when $P = 35$ and $b = 5$.
\newline

    

(c) Solve.
\newline
    (i) (d) $10x = 5$

        

(e) $7x - 3 = 2x + 11$

        

(f) $3(2x - 1) = 27$

        

(g) Rearrange $T = 5(p + 2)$ to make $p$ the subject.
\newline

    

(h) Solve the simultaneous equations.
\newline
\\
    You must show all your working.
\newline
    \begin{align*}
        3x - y &= 22 \\
        x + 2y &= 5
    \end{align*}

content.content:
$P = 4ab + 3b^2$\\
    Work out the value of $a$ when $P = 35$ and $b = 5$.
\newline

Solve.

Rearrange $T = 5(p + 2)$ to make $p$ the subject.
\newline

Solve the simultaneous equations.
\newline
\\
    You must show all your working.
\newline
    \begin{align*}
        3x - y &= 22 \\
        x + 2y &= 5
    \end{align*}

tex:

    Work out the bearing of town E from town D.

(f) The diagram shows three towns, P, Q and R.

\newline
    (i) (g) Complete the statement.
\newline
\\
        Town { } is due west of town { }.
\newline

(h) PQ and QR are two sides of a regular decagon.
\newline
\\
        Work out angle PQR.
\newline

qtype: estimation, rounding, ordering numbers, limits of accuracy, decimal and fraction operations
tex:

\newline
    $\dfrac{9.78 + 31.562}{0.381 \times 5.09}$

    

(b) Write these numbers in order, smallest first.
\newline
    $\dfrac{22}{7} \qquad 3.142 \qquad \dfrac{333}{106} \qquad 3.1416$
    {} < {} < {} < {}

(c) The length, $p$ cm, of a pencil is 9.8 cm, correct to 2 significant figures.
\newline
\\
    Complete the statement about the value of $p$.
\newline
    {} $\le p <$ {}

(d) Calculate $3.142 - \dfrac{52163}{16604}$.
\newline
\\
    Give your answer in standard form correct to 2 significant figures.
\newline
